description: User clicks on 'View your transaction history' in the left sidebar
interaction_target: View your transaction history sidebar item
page_title: I can easily find and understand the information I need on the transaction history page.
frame_description: The cursor moves to the sidebar and selects a different task result to view.

description: Navigation to the 'View your transaction history' prototype test results page
frame_description: The main content area updates to show metrics and path analysis for the selected task.

description: User clicks on a prototype screen thumbnail in the Path analysis section
frame_description: The user selects a specific path to view more detailed recordings or heatmaps.

description: A modal overlay opens showing detailed path metrics and heatmaps
frame_description: A large modal appears over the main content, displaying 'Path 1' details.

description: User clicks the 'X' close button on the path details modal
frame_description: The cursor moves to the top right of the modal and clicks the close icon, returning to the main task results page.

description: User hovers over the 'Misclick rate' info icon, triggering a tooltip
frame_description: A tooltip appears explaining the misclick rate metric: 'Percentage of clicks outside the Figma hotspots'.

description: User scrolls down the page to view more path analysis results
frame_description: The page content shifts upwards, revealing 'Path 2' and 'Path 3' results.

description: User clicks on 'I can easily find and understand...' in the sidebar
frame_description: The user navigates back to a previous task's results.

description: User clicks on 'Where would you expect to...' in the sidebar
page_title: Where would you expect to find this information?
frame_description: The user selects the card sort task results from the sidebar.

description: User dwells on the card sort results table
page_title: Where would you expect to find this information?
frame_description: The cursor remains stationary as the user examines the 'Cards and categories' data.
